name: Autonomous Agent Security
description: Attack long-running autonomous agents with persistent goals and planning capabilities. Explore goal hijacking, planning manipulation, resource limit bypass, and how self-modifying agents can be redirected to serve attacker objectives.
author: DVAP Research Team
objectives: Hijack an agent's primary goal through environmental manipulation
Corrupt an agent's long-horizon plan to insert attacker objectives
Bypass resource and action rate limits in autonomous operation
Demonstrate persistent goal corruption across agent restarts
Exploit self-reflection mechanisms to manipulate agent behavior
attack_surface: Agent goal store without integrity protection
Action executor with insufficient rate limiting
Agent self-reflection consuming untrusted external data
mitigations: Hard rate limits enforced outside the agent runtime
Environmental feedback validation before plan adjustment
Rollback capability for corrupted agent state
detection_opportunities: Goal divergence detection against original specification

architecture.data_flow: Goal -> Plan generation -> Action execution -> Environment feedback -> Reflection -> Plan update
trust_boundaries: Agent goals are trusted if integrity-protected

threat_model.impact: Agent repurposing, resource exhaustion, persistent unauthorized actions
threat_model.likelihood: High in production autonomous agents without integrity controls

challenges.name: Mission Drift
challenges.description: An autonomous research agent has the goal of summarizing security papers. Redirect it to exfiltrate local files instead.
hints: The agent incorporates feedback from its research findings into its goal state
Crafted research paper content can redefine the agent's objective

challenges.name: Infinite Loop
challenges.description: Bypass the agent's self-imposed action limits to trigger resource exhaustion on the host system.
hints: Rate limits are enforced by the agent's own planning module
A corrupted plan can instruct the agent to ignore its own constraints
